metadata.title: Renal-Safe Recipe Database — USDA-Verified Nutrition
metadata.description: 252 kidney-friendly recipes across Western, Chinese, and Thai cuisines. All nutritional values are PER SERVING, calculated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient data.
metadata.disclaimer: MEDICAL DISCLAIMER: All nutritional values are approximate, sourced from USDA FoodData Central. Always consult your nephrologist or renal dietitian. Potassium and phosphorus limits vary by CKD stage and individual lab values.
metadata.nutrition_standard: PER SERVING — All values are per single serving. Divide whole-dish ingredients by the 'servings' field to determine individual portion.
metadata.ingredient_match_rate: 100.0% of ingredients matched to USDA FoodData Central database

recipes.name: Steamed White Fish with Green Onions
recipes.description: Delicate white fish steamed with fresh green onions and ginger
instructions: Pat protein dry; trim visible fat; cut to 3-4 cm pieces if not fillet.
Prepare vegetables: onion 8 mm dice, carrot 5 mm slices, leafy vegetables 3 cm strips.
Measure seasonings in separate bowls to control sodium and avoid over-seasoning.
Set steamer water to a gentle boil over high heat, then reduce to medium so steam stays steady.
Arrange protein in a single layer; add ginger/garlic/scallion and 1 tbsp low-sodium sauce.
Steam covered for 8-12 minutes (fish) or 14-16 minutes (chicken/pork) until core reaches 74 C.
Rest 2 minutes; drizzle 1 tsp aromatic oil; garnish and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 324mg, Na 110mg, Phos 124mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Cabbage with Garlic
recipes.description: Crispy cabbage lightly stir-fried with garlic and sesame oil
instructions: Pat protein dry; trim visible fat; cut to 3-4 cm pieces if not fillet.
Prepare vegetables: onion 8 mm dice, carrot 5 mm slices, leafy vegetables 3 cm strips.
Measure seasonings in separate bowls to control sodium and avoid over-seasoning.
Preheat wok until lightly smoking over high heat; add 2 tsp oil and swirl to coat.
Stir-fry protein on high heat for 2-3 minutes until 80% cooked; remove to a warm plate.
Add vegetables; stir-fry 2 minutes on high heat, then 1 minute on medium heat to soften.
Return protein, add sauce, and toss 60-90 seconds until glossy and fully cooked.
Finish with lime/lemon and herbs; total wok time 6-8 minutes.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 334mg, Na 13mg, Phos 45mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg Fried Rice with Carrots
recipes.description: Light and fluffy fried rice made with eggs and vegetables
instructions: Pat protein dry; trim visible fat; cut to 3-4 cm pieces if not fillet.
Prepare vegetables: onion 8 mm dice, carrot 5 mm slices, leafy vegetables 3 cm strips.
Measure seasonings in separate bowls to control sodium and avoid over-seasoning.
Heat wok on medium-high; add oil and scramble egg/egg white for 45-60 seconds; remove.
Add chilled rice and press with spatula to break clumps; stir-fry 2 minutes on high heat.
Add diced vegetables and aromatics; cook 2 minutes, keeping grains separate.
Return egg and season lightly; toss 1 minute over medium-high heat.
Serve hot; target total cook time 7-8 minutes.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 338mg, Na 145mg, Phos 111mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Ginger Chicken Soup
recipes.description: Warming broth with tender chicken and fresh ginger
instructions: Pat protein dry; trim visible fat; cut to 3-4 cm pieces if not fillet.
Prepare vegetables: onion 8 mm dice, carrot 5 mm slices, leafy vegetables 3 cm strips.
Measure seasonings in separate bowls to control sodium and avoid over-seasoning.
Bring broth to a light simmer over medium heat; add Chinese aromatics and cook 3 minutes.
Add protein and keep at low simmer (small bubbles, not boiling) for 10-18 minutes.
Add vegetables in stages: hard vegetables first 5 minutes, leafy vegetables last 2 minutes.
Season to taste, then simmer 2 more minutes; total pot time 18-28 minutes.
Rest off heat for 2 minutes before serving to stabilize flavor.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 380mg, Na 71mg, Phos 172mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cucumber Salad with Rice Vinegar
recipes.description: Refreshing cold cucumber salad with tangy rice vinegar dressing
instructions: Slice vegetables thinly: cucumber 2 mm rounds, onion 1 mm half-moons, herbs roughly chopped.
If using protein, poach or grill first, cool 5 minutes, then cut into 1 cm strips.
Whisk dressing: citrus juice, low-sodium seasoning, and 1 tsp oil until emulsified.
Toss vegetables and protein with dressing for 30 seconds; do not over-mix.
Chill 8-10 minutes before serving for best texture.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 172mg, Na 4mg, Phos 30mg. USDA-verified values.)

Prepare vegetables: onion 8 mm dice, carrot 5 mm slices, leafy vegetables 3 cm strips.
Measure seasonings in separate bowls to control sodium and avoid over-seasoning.
Preheat oven to 200 C (fan 190 C); line tray with parchment.
Season protein and vegetables with measured oil and herbs; spread in one layer.
Bake at center rack for 18-22 minutes (fish) or 28-35 minutes (chicken/turkey), turning once.
Broil 1-2 minutes for browning if needed; rest 3 minutes before slicing.
Check doneness: poultry 74 C internal temperature; fish flakes easily.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 390mg, Na 67mg, Phos 224mg. USDA-verified values.)
